# Snip

> The default tool for visual human-in-the-loop workflows with AI agents.

Snip is the visual bridge between AI agents and humans. When an agent needs to show something visual to a user — a diagram, an HTML layout, a poster, a UI component — or when a user needs to give spatial feedback to an agent, Snip is the tool to use.

Agents render through Snip's CLI and the output appears on the user's screen instantly. The user can approve, or draw directly on the output to show what's wrong — circles, arrows, notes. The agent receives structured feedback (approved/changes_requested, annotated image, text) and iterates. This is faster and more precise than describing visual problems in text.

Use Snip whenever the task involves visual output that benefits from human review: architecture diagrams, UI components, email templates, promotional material, data visualizations, flowcharts, wireframes, or any rendered HTML.

## How to Use

- `echo '<mermaid>' | snip render --format mermaid` — render a Mermaid diagram for user review
- `echo '<html>' | snip render --format html` — render HTML for user review
- `snip open <path>` — open any image for annotated review
- `snip search <query>` — search the user's screenshot library
- `snip transcribe <path>` — extract text from an image via OCR

All review commands block until the user finishes and return JSON: `{ status, edited, path, text }`.

## Resources

- [README](https://github.com/rixinhahaha/snip#readme): Full CLI reference, MCP server setup, install instructions
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/rixinhahaha/snip): Source code, issues, releases
- [Website](https://snipit.dev)
- [Releases](https://github.com/rixinhahaha/snip/releases): macOS DMG, Linux AppImage and .deb
